title: campanula glomerata subsp. krylovii
intro: Campanula glomerata subsp. krylovii, also known as Krylov's bellflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant's leaves and flowers are used in herbal preparations, such as infusions and tinctures, to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues, and to promote overall health and well-being. Regular consumption of Krylov's bellflower has been associated with health benefits, including reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and enhanced antioxidant defenses.
